methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $12M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Defense & Security. Range: $60M - $500M.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use

private.name: Tenna Systems
private.rationale: Same sector (Defense & Security), same stage (Seed), post-money $60M

data.narrative:
Bone AI is a seed-stage Defense & Security company that has raised $12M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a peer set of 4 same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables—three Series A firms (Stark Defence at $500M, Vatn Systems at $300M, Ares Industries at $150M) and one seed-stage peer (Tenna Systems at $60M)—alongside broader market context including 2 public comps and 8 private peers in the frontier-defense space.

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis uses the $12M raised as a proxy base value and applies a typical 2.5x seed-to-next-stage multiplier, implying a $30M valuation. Peer-median analysis yields $225M, derived from the median across the four comparables with a range of $60M to $500M. The peer-median result reflects higher stage-adjusted valuations in the peer set, skewed toward Series A assets.

The triangulated valuation range is $26M (floor) to $259M (ceiling), with a median estimate of $128M. The wide dispersion reflects genuine uncertainty around Bone AI's relative technical maturity, market traction, and defensibility versus the peer set. Stage-progression anchors conservatively near the raised capital; peer-median suggests significantly higher entry-stage multiples are supported in defense-tech currently. Resolution of this gap depends on visibility into product development milestones, customer validation, and competitive positioning within the Defense & Security vertical.
